acquisition method: Two common challenges in 15N quantification using DDA acquisition and quantification
cleavage agent: excised, trypsin digested, and analyzed by liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry
collision energy: For a Q-Exactive HF, we recommend the “(N)CE” column should be “27” for all rows
fractionation method: separated by the SDS-PAGE gel with a very short run
fragmentation method: Peptides were fragmented with higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD)
instrument: analyzed by liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC–MS) as described in and on a Q-Exactive HF instrument
labeling: we have previously employed PRM to phosphopeptides in label-free samples
